title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an exhaustive asynchronous state machine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.
Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
